_readme: Master MCP stack for ~/.dev-infra. Do not put real tokens here — use ${ENV_VAR} references. The install-mcp script reads this file and writes to the right location for each tool.

github._why: Create issues, open PRs, check CI status, read repo structure — without leaving the terminal session.
github._requires: GITHUB_TOKEN env var (personal access token with repo scope). Generate at github.com/settings/tokens.

filesystem._why: Gives agents safe, sandboxed read/write access to your projects directory. Without this, agents can only see files passed to them explicitly.
filesystem._requires: Nothing. The allowed paths are set in args — adjust PROJECTS_DIR to your actual projects folder.

context7._why: Pulls live, version-specific library documentation into context on demand. Eliminates the single biggest source of AI coding errors: stale API knowledge from training data.
context7._requires: Nothing for free tier (rate-limited by IP). Optional: set CONTEXT7_API_KEY for higher limits — get one at context7.com/dashboard.

sequential-thinking._why: Structures the orchestrator's multi-step reasoning. Instead of one-shot plans, the agent works through problems step-by-step, can revise previous steps, and branches when needed. Especially valuable for complex version planning.

playwright._why: Browser automation for end-to-end testing after each version. The orchestrator can spin up a browser, navigate to your dev server, and verify the acceptance criterion is visually met — without you having to do it manually every time.
playwright._requires: Node 18+. First run: npx playwright install chromium
